fields.name: SRAMER
fields.description: SRAM1 erase This bit can be set by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the ERASEKEY field of the RAMCFG_M1ERKEYR register. Setting this bit starts the SRAM1 erase. This bit is automatically cleared by hardware at the end of the erase operation.

fields.name: WSC
fields.description: SRAM1 wait state configuration This field is used to program the number of wait states inserted on the AHB when reading the SRAM1, depending on its access time. ... Note: Before entering Stop 1 mode software must set SRAM1 wait states to at least 1.

fields.name: SRAMBUSY
fields.description: SRAM busy with erase operation. Note: Depending on the SRAM, the erase operation can be performed due to software request, system reset if the enabled by user option, tamper detection or RDP regression. Refer to Table38.

fields.name: ERASEKEY
fields.description: Erase write protection key The following steps are required to unlock the write protection of the SRAMER bit in the RAMCFG_MxCR register. Write 0xCA into ERASEKEY[7:0] Write 0x53 into ERASEKEY[7:0] Note: Writing a wrong key reactivates the write protection.

fields.name: ALE
fields.description: SRAM2 parity fail address latch enable

fields.name: SRAMER
fields.description: SRAM2 erase This bit can be set by software only after writing the unlock sequence in the ERASEKEY field of the RAMCFG_M2ERKEYR register. Setting this bit starts the SRAM2 erase. This bit is automatically cleared by hardware at the end of the erase operation.

fields.name: WSC
fields.description: SRAM2 wait state configuration This field is used to program the number of wait states inserted on the AHB when reading the SRAM2, depending on its access time. ... Note: Before entering Stop 1 mode software must set SRAM2 wait states to at least 1.

fields.name: PEIE
fields.description: Parity error interrupt enable

fields.name: PENMI
fields.description: Parity error NMI. This bit is set by software and cleared only by a global RAMCFG reset Note: When PENMI bit is set, the RAMCFG maskable interrupt is not generated for a parity error whatever PEIE bit value.

fields.name: PED
fields.description: Parity error detected

fields.name: SRAMBUSY
fields.description: SRAM2 busy with erase operation. Note: Depending on the SRAM2, the erase operation can be performed due to software request, system reset if the enabled by user option, tamper detection or RDP regression. Refer to Table38.

registers.name: M2PEAR
registers.description: RAMCFG SRAM2 parity error address register

fields.name: PEA
fields.description: Parity error SRAM word aligned address offset.PEA[1:0] read 0b00. When ALE bit is set in RAMCFG_M2CR register, this field is updated when PED and CPED are zero and a new parity error is detected, with the SRAM word aligned address offset corresponding to the parity error.

fields.name: ID
fields.description: Parity error AHB bus master ID. When ALE bit is set in RAMCFG_M2CR register, this field is updated when PED and CPED are zero and a new parity error is detected, with: Others: reserved

fields.name: BYTE
fields.description: Byte parity error flag. When ALE bit is set in RAMCFG_M2CR register, this field is updated when PED and CPED are zero and a new parity error is detected, with: 1xxx: parity error detected on fourth byte in word aligned address x1xx: parity error detected on third byte in word aligned address xx1x: parity error detected on second byte in word aligned address xxx1: parity error detected on first byte in word aligned address

fields.name: CPED
fields.description: Clear parity error detect bit Writing 1 to this bit clears the PED bit in RAMCFG_M2ISR. Reading this bit returns the value of the RAMCFG_M2ISR PED bit.

fields.name: P0WP
fields.description: SRAM2 1-Kbyte write protect page y write protection These bits are set by software and cleared only by a system reset.

fields.name: ERASEKEY
fields.description: Erase write protection key The following steps are required to unlock the write protection of the SRAMER bit in the RAMCFG_MxCR register. Write 0xCA into ERASEKEY[7:0] Write 0x53 into ERASEKEY[7:0] Note: Writing a wrong key reactivates the write protection.
